relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks food-boundary instruction to Moses and Aaron.
Israel is taught which living creatures may be eaten.
Hoof and cud mark the permitted land animals.
The camel does not fit the sign.
The hyrax does not fit the sign.
The hare does not fit the sign.
The pig does not fit the sign.
Their flesh and carcass are avoided as ritual boundary.
Fin and scale mark permitted water creatures.
Waters without fin and scale are ritually avoided.
Their flesh is not eaten.
The water boundary is repeated.
Certain birds are ritually avoided.
Kites and falcons are named.
Ravens are named.
Ostrich, nighthawk, gull, and hawk are named.
Owls and water birds are named.
More carrion and water birds are named.
Stork, heron, hoopoe, and bat are named.
Most winged swarmers are avoided.
Leaping insects are permitted.
Locusts and their kinds are named.
Other winged swarmers are avoided.
Touching carcass brings temporary uncleanness.
Carrying carcass requires washing and waiting.
Incomplete hoof/cud signs mark ritual uncleanness.
Pawed animals are ritually unclean when dead.
The carrier washes and waits until evening.
Certain ground swarmers are named unclean.
Lizard kinds are named.
Their dead bodies transfer temporary uncleanness.
Objects touched by carcass are washed and wait.
Earthen vessels are broken when contaminated.
Food and drink may become ritually unclean.
Ovens and hearths may be broken down.
Springs and cisterns remain clean.
Dry seed remains clean.
Wetted seed can receive uncleanness.
An edible animal’s carcass brings temporary uncleanness.
Eating or carrying carcass requires washing and waiting.
Earth swarmers are not eaten.
Crawling and many-footed creatures are avoided.
The people guard their souls/lives through ritual distinction.
Be holy, for YHWH is holy.
The One who brought Israel up from Egypt calls them into holiness.
This is instruction for beast, bird, water life, and earth swarmers.
The purpose is distinction between clean and unclean, edible and not eaten.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 11 is not disgust; it is covenant discernment around eating, touching, washing, waiting, and remembering that the Liberator calls Israel into holiness.
